quizzes.d: Core concepts in mechanics, forces, and motion

q.t: What unit is used to measure force?
Which of these is a vector quantity?
What does the speedometer in a car measure?
According to Newton's Third Law, when you push against a wall the wall pushes back with what force?
What is the SI unit of pressure?
A car accelerates from 0 to 60 m/s in 10 seconds. What is its acceleration?
Which law states that the pressure and volume of a gas are inversely proportional at constant temperature?
At what angle must a projectile be launched to achieve maximum range (ignoring air resistance)?
What is the escape velocity from Earth's surface approximately?
In simple harmonic motion, where is the restoring force greatest?
a: Halfway between equilibrium and maximum displacement

quizzes.d: Explore electric circuits, fields, and magnetic phenomena

q.t: What is the unit of electrical resistance?
In a series circuit, what happens to the total resistance when you add another resistor?
What does an ammeter measure?
What type of current is produced by most power stations and delivered to homes?
Ohm's Law states that voltage equals:
What is the relationship between the frequency and wavelength of electromagnetic radiation?
A transformer steps voltage up by a factor of 10. By what factor does the current change?
What is the direction of the magnetic force on a positive charge moving to the right in a magnetic field pointing out of the page?
The Hall effect demonstrates that current in a conductor is carried by:
a: Charge carriers whose sign can be determined

q.t: Maxwell's equations unify which two fundamental forces?

quizzes.d: Elements, compounds, reactions, and the periodic table

q.t: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
How many elements make up water (H₂O)?
What is the pH of a neutral solution at 25°C?
Which gas makes up the largest proportion of Earth's atmosphere?
What type of bond involves the sharing of electrons between atoms?
In the periodic table, elements in the same group (column) share what property?
a: The same number of valence electrons

q.t: What is the IUPAC name for the compound with formula CH₃COOH?
Which type of reaction involves a substance combining with oxygen to produce heat and light?
What is the hybridization of carbon in methane (CH₄)?
Le Chatelier's principle states that if stress is applied to a system at equilibrium, the system will:
a: Collapse in the direction of the stress
Shift in a direction that counteracts the stress
Increase the rate of both reactions equally

quizzes.d: Life at the cellular level — DNA, heredity, and cell processes

q.t: What organelle is known as the powerhouse of the cell?
What molecule carries genetic information in most organisms?
How many chromosomes does a normal human body cell contain?
What is the process by which plants convert sunlight into chemical energy?
Which type of cell division produces gametes (sex cells)?
What is the term for a segment of DNA that codes for a specific protein?
If a parent has blood type AB and another has type O, what blood types are possible in their offspring?
What is the role of tRNA during protein synthesis?
a: Carries the genetic code from nucleus to ribosome
Brings amino acids to the ribosome

q.t: In which stage of the cell cycle does DNA replication occur?
What mutation type causes a frameshift in the reading frame of a gene?

quizzes.d: Planets, stars, galaxies, and the universe beyond

q.t: Which planet is the largest in our solar system?
What is the name of our galaxy?
Approximately how long does light from the Sun take to reach Earth?
What is a light-year a measurement of?
Which mission was the first to land humans on the Moon?
What type of stellar remnant will our Sun eventually become?
What does the Hubble constant measure?
a: The age of the universe only
The rate of expansion of the universe
The distance to the nearest star
The mass of the Milky Way

q.t: What phenomenon causes the apparent shift in wavelength of light from a receding star?
The Chandrasekhar limit (~1.4 solar masses) is the maximum mass of which type of stellar remnant?
What is the name of the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way?

quizzes.d: Geology, atmosphere, weather, and the forces that shape our planet

q.t: What is the outermost layer of the Earth called?
What scale is used to measure the magnitude of earthquakes?
What type of rock is formed from cooled lava or magma?
What gas in the atmosphere primarily absorbs harmful UV radiation from the Sun?
What drives plate tectonic movement?
What is the name of the supercontinent that existed approximately 300 million years ago?
Which type of tectonic boundary involves two plates moving toward each other?
What is the process by which rocks are broken down by physical and chemical means at Earth's surface?
The Milankovitch cycles describe variations in Earth's orbital parameters that influence what?
At what depth does the Mohorovičić discontinuity (Moho) typically occur beneath continental crust?

quizzes.d: Anatomy, physiology, and how the human body works

q.t: How many bones are in the adult human body?
What is the largest organ in the human body?
Which blood type is considered the universal donor for red blood cells?
What is the normal resting heart rate range for a healthy adult?
Which part of the brain is primarily responsible for balance and coordination?
What molecule transports oxygen in red blood cells?
In which organ does the majority of nutrient absorption occur during digestion?
What is the primary function of the lymphatic system?
a: Return interstitial fluid to bloodstream and support immunity

q.t: Which enzyme is responsible for unwinding the DNA double helix during replication?
What is the approximate total length of DNA in a single human cell if fully stretched out?

quizzes.d: The people and breakthroughs that changed science forever

q.t: Who developed the theory of general relativity?
What did Alexander Fleming discover in 1928?
Who first described the heliocentric model of the solar system in detail in the 16th century?
